chapter goal: Compute with conics using homogeneous matrices: split degenerates, branch safely, intersect conics with lines and conics, handle complex roots, and impose one tangent constraint.
concept inventory: rank-2 degenerate conic A = g h^T + h g^T and skew correction to rank one
rank-1 double line and the need to inspect a nonzero matrix entry
line-conic intersection as splitting M_l^T A M_l
conic-conic intersection via the determinant cubic of a pencil
complex conjugate roots and projective real representatives
four points plus one tangent line through fixed points of an involution
decision-tree checks for ranks, pivots, roots, and residuals

library routing table.why: direct ranks, null vectors, skew corrections, and residuals
verifies A + M_{g x h} = 2 g h^T exactly
durable static figures with equal aspect and contours
the real/tangent/complex transition is a parameter phenomenon
keeps branch triggers and residuals auditable

proof-visualization strategy: Use an exact skew identity, a branch-forcing double-line path, and an implementation decision graph instead of a decorative route map.

visual sequence.inspection target: the symmetric union matrix and the skew-corrected rank-one matrix encode the same quadratic form
a closed path of double-line matrices cannot choose a continuous vector representative without a branch
secant, tangent, and exterior lines differ by the split becoming real, double, or complex
move the line through an ellipse and watch the discriminant cross zero
a determinant cubic selects line-pair members of the conic pencil
complex conjugate points can join to a real line
two fixed points on the line generate two tangent conics through four points
every recipe has a branch trigger and an invariant
